resources.string: Shows refresh progress and results for GitHub project tracking
Checked %1$d/%2$d · pre-release updates %3$d · stable updates %4$d · failed %5$d
Notification permission is off, so the test notification was skipped
Add at least one tracked project first
Sent to the Android built-in downloader
Sent to the system default handler
Selected downloader unavailable. Falling back to the system default handler.
No update APK available to load
This release has no usable APK
Sent %1$s. Tracking will be added automatically after install or update finishes.
This project and app are already tracked locally, so it was skipped automatically.
No valid GitHub link was detected
The target release contains no usable APK
This repository has no usable release
The shared link is missing a release tag
This repository has no stable release
Shizuku is not running, so KeiOS cannot manage this install
KeiOS has no Shizuku permission, so it cannot manage this install
The Shizuku remote process has no INSTALL_PACKAGES capability
Install reported success without a package name
Branches appear after workflows are loaded
This repository has no active workflow to show
This workflow has no run to show
This run has no artifact to show
nightly.link could not read workflows from the public GitHub page. Confirm the repository is public and has .github/workflows, or switch to GitHub API Token.
nightly.link could not read a run with downloadable artifacts. Confirm the selected branch has a successful run, or switch to GitHub API Token.
nightly.link could not read a downloadable artifact. Confirm the workflow uploads artifacts with actions/upload-artifact and the artifact is still retained; GitHub API Token is recommended.
Actions load failed: %1$s\nnightly.link depends on a public repository, a successful run, and a retained artifact. Switch to GitHub API Token for complete Actions data.
GitHub token is required to download Actions artifacts
This run is still running. Status refreshes automatically after completion, and manual refresh is available.
Enter a token in GitHub fetch strategy first
This artifact can be downloaded after the run completes
A GitHub token is required to download Actions artifacts
The artifact is missing a download URL and repository information
GitHub Actions artifact download returned no redirect URL
GitHub public API found no matching workflow: %1$s
GitHub Actions token is invalid or expired
GitHub Actions guest API is rate limited. Try again later or enter a token.
GitHub Actions API is rate limited
GitHub Actions API access was denied%1$s
The repository or GitHub Actions resource does not exist, or the current token lacks access
The GitHub Actions artifact has expired
nightly.link cannot directly download raw APK artifact %1$s. Enter a GitHub API Token or switch to the GitHub API Token path.
nightly.link did not read workflow artifacts
nightly.link did not read downloadable artifacts. Confirm the latest successful workflow run uploaded artifacts and the artifacts are still retained; GitHub API Token is recommended.
nightly.link or the public GitHub page denied access; GitHub API Token is recommended.
nightly.link or the public GitHub page could not find the Actions resource; GitHub API Token is recommended.
nightly.link reports that the artifact has expired. Select a newer run or switch to GitHub API Token.
nightly.link or the public GitHub page is rate limited. Try again later or switch to GitHub API Token.
nightly.link or the public GitHub page read failed: %1$s
%1$s currently has no downloadable assets except Source code
%1$s currently has no directly downloadable assets
Enter a package name or select a local app first
Default tracking for this project restored
Unable to open permission page. Grant it manually in system settings.
The import file contains no valid tracked projects
Import completed: added %1$d, updated %2$d, unchanged %3$d, ignored %4$d
Long-press the overview card to choose which entries appear while expanded. The collapsed state keeps the key summary.
No items yet. Add one first.
Version format cannot be compared precisely
This project may only have pre-release builds for now
No APK found or all assets loaded manually
Open release / long-press to load all
Reading downloadable files in this release
APK files are prioritized. If no APK is found, other assets are shown as fallback.
Using guest API. You can add a token anytime.
Token filled. Replace it here if needed.
When empty, guest API is used automatically and works for light tracking. Add a local token when the project count grows or rate limits appear. The token is stored only in MMKV on this device.

GitHub API Token is shared by Releases and Actions. Release metadata and asset downloads need `Contents: Read`; Actions workflows, runs, artifacts, and artifact downloads need `Actions: Read`; private repositories also require repository access on the token.
Guest API is currently available, with a lower quota. Add a token when tracking more projects.
The current token was accepted by GitHub API. Repository-level private access still depends on token permissions.
API strategy uses Releases API directly. Start with guest mode for first use; a dedicated token is more stable for long-term tracking across many repositories.
API strategy reuses the same GitHub token. Release reads need Contents: Read; Actions artifact reads and downloads need Actions: Read.
Unable to open GitHub create page
Create a dedicated Fine-grained token for KeiOS. This keeps the permission surface smaller than reusing a classic token.
Atom requires no token and fits public repositories and lightweight checks. API is better for stability, asset reads, and private repository support.
Current tracked repositories will be used for comparison, up to 6 samples. No usable samples are available yet.

Compares release snapshots, release assets, release notes, APK manifests, cached retests, APK package scans, and package-to-repository scans to show Atom/API timing, hit rate, and capability differences.
Success count wins first. When success is tied, an average time at least 15% faster is clearly ahead; smaller gaps are close.
Downloader, system sharing, and post-install tracking linkage
Current project, import/export, and starred repository batch import
After the selected interval, background and page refreshes treat the cache as stale. Shorter intervals send more requests; 3-6 hours fits larger tracking lists.
Check latest pre-release for every tracked item
Also checks the latest pre-release for each project. This does not automatically recommend installing it.
Ignore `armeabi-v7a`, `armeabi`, `x86_64`, and `x86`; when arm64-v8a exists, ignore universal as well.
Reads real versionName and versionCode from remote APK Manifest data; large tracking lists may spend a little more time parsing.
Off by default. When enabled, traffic, fork sync, and security endpoints are read for deeper checks. Token mode gives fuller data and uses more requests.
Used for opening direct download links; follows system default by default.
Allows sharing GitHub links from other apps to KeiOS, automatically resolving release APKs and entering install linkage.
Choose a sheet-led flow or a quieter live notification / island flow.
When enabled, single-APK share-import linkage downloads and installs through KeiOS with Shizuku.
KeiOS manages installation, so the external installer choice is temporarily locked.
Share actions can be sent directly to installers that support download-while-install.
No supported installer detected, so linkage stays disabled by default.
Shows health score and APK trust checks in tracked cards, with full Release Notes in the more menu.
Uses local check results to estimate whether a project needs attention.
Shows install hints from filename, ABI, build type, and file format.
Number of tracked projects saved locally on this device
Restore the default KeiOS tracking item to prevent it from missing or being deleted accidentally after updates.
Current KeiOS is already in the tracking list
Export backup; preview smart merge results before import.
Sends a simulated #1424 live notification / island to test styling, permission, and actions.
Import shows a preview first, then smart-merges by “owner/repo + package name”: same IDs are overwritten, new items are appended, and existing items missing from the import file are kept.
Global pre-release checks only discover versions; each item’s “Prefer pre-release version” decides the recommended target.
Aggressive filtering keeps arm64 first. Universal is filtered only when the same release already has arm64-v8a.
“Share to installer” only affects share actions. “Downloader” only affects direct link opening.

This option only affects “Share” actions and defaults to no linkage. Currently recognizes `com.rosan.installer.x.revived` and `io.github.vvb2060.packageinstaller`, allowing direct share to installers that support download-while-install.

The downloader list first scans candidate apps that support `ACTION_VIEW` + `CATEGORY_BROWSABLE`, then prioritizes entries that look like downloaders. “Android built-in downloader” uses Android DownloadManager directly.
Use your stars, another user’s public stars, or a public Star List URL.
The current GitHub API token will read /user/starred
Importing your stars requires a GitHub API token in the fetch strategy sheet
Choose a source and load the list. The preview lets you filter and deselect items.
Loaded %1$d repositories, %2$d importable, %3$d already tracked, %4$d selected.
Add a GitHub API token in the fetch strategy sheet.
Enter a username, profile URL, or stars URL to read public stars.
Paste a GitHub profile stars page, /stars/..., or /stars/.../lists/... URL.
Example: ghp_xxx...; configure the token in the GitHub fetch strategy sheet.
Reads /user/starred for the account behind the current token. Configure the token in GitHub fetch strategy.
Enter a GitHub username or profile URL here. For a Star List category, switch to List and paste the full URL.
Paste the full Star List or Stars URL from the browser address bar. A numeric ID alone cannot locate the public list.
Found %1$d public Star Lists. Choose a category to continue.
Found %1$d public categories for category-based import.
Looks like an Android project and is selected by default.
APK signals are unclear. Check releases before importing.
Looks closer to desktop, CLI, or server software.
The repository is archived or a fork. Import carefully.
Import %1$d repositories. %2$d have verified APKs and %3$d are unverified.
%1$d repositories need extra care because they may have no APK or look like other-platform projects.
These repositories have no reliable APK evidence yet and may need manual correction after import.
These repositories have weaker Android signals or special maintenance status. Review them one by one.
These repositories are importable, and APK discovery has not been loaded yet.
These repositories expose APKs in their latest stable release.
%1$d repositories are selected. Leaving will require loading and selecting them again.
Adjust the search query or switch filters.
Use a repository or release URL. The project URL can also be scanned from the package name.
These repositories were verified against the same package name. Select one to fill the draft.
Leave empty to track only repository version. Filling a package name later or installing the matching app will link refresh automatically.
The package name matches local install state. It can also be scanned from the latest stable APK.
Optional. Binding a local app enables installed-version comparison and install-time refresh.
Checks pre-release versions for this project and prefers them when available. The global switch remains unchanged.
When enabled, the download entry always shows. The target is selected by comparing stable and pre-release publish dates.
Checks this project’s recommended Actions Run on the refresh interval and sends a live notification / island when a newer run appears.
Controls whether this project reads real versionName and versionCode from the remote APK Manifest. It can follow global settings or override them.
Items will be smart-merged and written to this device.
This file has no valid importable items yet.
The GitHub share entry is currently disabled. Open the GitHub page and enable share import linkage in check settings.
A local tracked item with the same repository and package already exists. This operation will skip adding automatically.
After installation finishes, KeiOS will detect the newly installed or updated app and continue adding tracking.
APK package name is identified. After installation finishes, KeiOS will link by package name and continue adding tracking.
%1$d local items already track this repository. Same-repository same-package items are deduplicated automatically.
No obvious score change in this refresh
Base score is 64; Good starts at 80, Review starts at 50, and lower scores enter Risk.
Lifecycle, activity, releases, assets, Actions, community files, local fit, fork sync, and deep profile signals all contribute.

Strong penalties come from archived or disabled repositories, failed checks, missing package names, missing stable releases, missing local installs, stale pushes, stale releases, missing Android assets, package mismatch, and forks clearly behind upstream.

Bonuses come from stable release detection, recent releases, available updates, Android APK assets, recent successful Actions, README and license coverage, package match, and active independently maintained forks.

Forks get a small initial penalty. Active upstream drift adds more penalty; active fork maintenance or continued work after an archived upstream offsets that impact. Pre-release recommendations lower stability confidence.
Deep-mode traffic and security are lightweight signals. The score drives overview priority and reminders; APK file trust comes from the asset card and APK detail view.
Refreshing release assets and release notes
This release has no displayable release notes.
No obvious risk signal detected for this artifact
Expand to read how version, API, ABI, components, and permissions map to install risk.
target API: Android behavior level the app targets
ABI: CPU architectures covered by native libraries
exported: component callable by other apps
permission: permission required for a component or capability
queries: packages or intents the app can discover
No signing certificate info was read
No components, queries, or intent filters were parsed
No native lib ABI directory found
This artifact was selected by the base rules.
Create a dedicated Fine-grained PAT for KeiOS. Release API needs `Contents: Read`; Actions artifact API needs `Actions: Read`; public repositories can also be tracked.
`Only select repositories` only limits additionally authorized private repositories under the current owner; public repositories are unaffected.
`Selected repositories` is limited to 50 manually selected repositories under the current owner.
If the organization requires approval or enables SSO, the token may need approval or SSO authorization before use.
Classic tokens are recommended only as fallback because their permission surface is usually larger.
Uses Atom feed and release pages. Lightweight, with stronger dependency on page structure.
No token required; good for public repositories
Depends more on page structure and is more fragile when pages change
Weaker support for private repositories and fine-grained metadata
Uses Releases API. More structured and better for asset reads and private repositories.
More stable metadata and direct prerelease detection
Reads more release details and handles asset downloads more reliably
Guest quota is low and can hit rate limits with many projects
Access is limited when the token expires or lacks permissions
Token optional; guest API is used when empty
Combines public GitHub pages with nightly.link for token-free public-repository Actions artifact access.
No token required, so public repositories can be tried immediately
Shared links use stable nightly.link URLs that guests can open
Depends on public GitHub pages and nightly.link service state
Best for latest successful runs; raw APK artifacts still need token fallback download
Public repository; workflow uploads artifacts; nightly.link can access the repository; zip artifacts are recommended
Uses GitHub Actions REST API for stable workflow, run, and artifact reads.
Reads more runs, statuses, branches, and artifact metadata
Supports private repositories and fuller download-history matching
Requires storing a GitHub token locally
Access is limited when the token expires, lacks permissions, or quota is exhausted
Fine-grained PAT needs Actions: Read; private repositories also need authorization for the target repository
